latest.title: Net Asset Value(s)
latest.content:
13 Mar 2026 11:27
RNS Number : 6202W
JPMorgan Emerging EMEA Securities
13 March 2026
JPMORGAN EMERGING EUROPE, MIDDLE EAST & AFRICA SECURITIES PLC (the 'Company')
Legal Entity Identifier: 549300II3MHI98ZLVH37
THE COMPANY ANNOUNCES THE UNAUDITED NET ASSET VALUE (NAV) As at: 12 March 2026
THE NET ASSET VALUE PER SHARE IN PENCE, INCLUDING INCOME WITH DEBT AT FAIR VALUE:
65.43
The Directors consider that in the absence of observable market data on its Russian investments resulting from the closure of the Moscow Exchange (MOEX) to overseas investors, there has been a material change to the market value of its Russian investments and therefore a fair value valuation methodology has been applied to those investments held. For its MOEX local stock, an alternative fair value adjustment has been applied to the last trade price on 25th February 2022. The price of these stocks has been determined by taking the live market price as at 25th February 2022 and applying a 99% provision for valuation. Similarly, for the American Depositary Receipts and Global Depositary Receipts an alternative fair value adjustment has been applied to the last trade price on 2nd March 2022 and a 99% provision for valuation applied.
Name of contact and telephone number for queries:
Paul Ainger 0044 207 742 6524
Name of authorised company official responsible for making this notification:
Paul Winship 0044 207 742 9815 - Company Secretary
Date: 13 March 2026

latest.title: Result of AGM
latest.content:
11 Mar 2026 16:17
RNS Number : 2848W
JPMorgan Emerging EMEA Securities
11 March 2026
LONDON STOCK EXCHANGE ANNOUNCEMENT
JPMorgan Emerging Europe, Middle East & Africa Securities plc
ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING 2026
RESULT OF POLL AT ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING
Legal Entity Identifier:
549300II3MHI98ZLVH37
Information disclosed in accordance with UKLR 6.4.13
Following the Annual General Meeting of the Company, which was held today the Board is pleased to announce that all of the ordinary resolutions put to shareholders were voted on by Poll and passed. In addition, one item of special business was voted on by Poll and passed which:
i) will allow the Company to make market purchases of up to 6,061,383 Ordinary shares representing 14.99% of the Company's issued share capital.
The full text of all the resolutions can be found in the Company's Annual Report and Accounts which is available for viewing at the National Storage Mechanism which can be located at https://data.fca.org.uk/#/nsm/nationalstoragemechanism and from the Company's website,
www.jpmeemeasecurities.com
A summary of proxy votes received, and the poll results will shortly be available on the Company's website, www.jpmeemeasecurities.com
11th March 2026
For further information, please contact:
Joel Clopon

JPMorgan Funds Limited, Secretary
0800 20 40 20 (or +44 1268 44 44 70)

company_30s.what_they_do: JEMA Ltd is a UK‑listed investment vehicle that owns stakes in a mix of European and Russian assets, primarily through direct holdings and fund‑level positions. The firm’s core activity is managing those investments, collecting dividends, and reporting net asset value to shareholders.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company sits at the intersection of Western investment and Russian markets, a niche that becomes valuable whenever geopolitical risk shifts. Its portfolio gives it exposure to sectors that can rebound sharply when sanctions ease or new trade agreements materialise, making it a potential catalyst play for those who can weather the volatility.
company_30s.current_state: JEMA has recently declared dividends and reported its net asset value, indicating it is generating cash flow. The latest filing shows it still holds a sizable Russian position, suggesting it is not yet divesting. The company is still in the midst of a regulatory review, with court cases in Russia that could affect its holdings. Overall, it appears to be a cash‑generating but politically exposed entity that has not yet re‑established a clear growth trajectory.

rns_narrative: The company’s most recent announcements focus on financial health: a fresh net asset value report, dividend declaration, and a set of results for the year. These filings suggest the business is still profitable and paying out to shareholders.

A separate thread of filings deals with legal exposure: Russian court cases, a list of the ten largest investments, and a disclosure of holdings that may be under sanctions. This indicates ongoing scrutiny and potential regulatory risk.

Taken together, the narrative is one of a company that is still operating and paying dividends but is under a cloud of legal and geopolitical uncertainty. There is no sign of a strategic pivot, but the company is actively managing its exposure.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – traders are largely silent but a handful of bullish voices hint at a post‑sanction rebound.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: WhiteHorse7 and a few others argue that the company’s exposure to Russian assets could pay off once sanctions lift, citing recent dividend activity as evidence the firm still has cash to deploy.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: No clear bearish narrative exists; the lack of negative chatter is itself a signal of contrarian potential.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has stayed flat: no new drama has surfaced, and the community is not yet moving in either direction.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is dead, meaning there is no current public buzz. This is a classic contrarian set‑up – the market has largely ignored the story.

near_term_catalysts: A scheduled Q3 earnings release could reveal whether the company has secured new funding or cleared a regulatory hurdle.
Any announcement that sanctions against Russian entities are being eased would lift the value of JEMA’s holdings.
A sudden spike in trading volume – especially if it hits a key support level identified on the chart – could signal capitulation is over and a bounce is imminent.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals to monitor over the next 2‑3 months include: (1) a positive earnings surprise or a new investment announcement; (2) a clear reversal on volume‑weighted average price, indicating institutional interest; and (3) any change in the status of the Russian court cases that could unlock asset value.
expect_next.timing_regime: The company appears to be in the early stages of a potential recovery. The APEX score suggests a compressed range, which normally means a breakout could occur soon. Confirmation will come once the company either clears a regulatory hurdle or shows a tangible uptick in share volume.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company’s holdings are cleared from sanctions or if it announces a new investment that unlocks value, the stock could rebound by 50‑100% from its current level. The mean‑reversion thesis relies on the fact that the market has already priced in a deep sell‑off, so any catalyst that restores confidence can trigger a sharp upside. Historically, similar deep‑crash setups have delivered double‑digit returns in the first few weeks of recovery.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The biggest risk is that the Russian court cases continue to drag on, keeping the company’s assets frozen. If sanctions remain, the dividend stream could dry up and the stock could stay in a low‑price corridor. A worst‑case scenario would see the company’s valuation collapse further if it is forced to liquidate assets at a loss.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 50‑100% if a catalyst triggers a bounce. Potential downside: limited to the current price level because the stock is already deeply discounted. The asymmetry is clear – the upside far outweighs the downside.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price breaks above the last resistance level with at least a 10% volume increase. Allocate 10‑15% of the discretionary portfolio to the position. Set a stop 15% below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a clear volume spike or a positive earnings surprise. Watch for the price to reach the 50‑day moving average; a bounce off this level could be a trigger.
playbook.if_passing: If the company is delisted, files for bankruptcy, or a court ruling permanently freezes its assets, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: JEMA is a deeply discounted, geopolitically exposed investment that could rebound sharply once sanctions lift or a regulatory win occurs, offering a high‑reward, low‑downside play for contrarian traders.
